chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: What's up guys, you are back in 100 days of code  and today I will tell you about function arguments  that how can arguments be passed to function  we said that function is used to separate code  but there are many ways by which we can pass arguments to function

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: we can pass some arguments to function as a tuple  and we can pass as a dictionary  let's go to the computer screen and see how default arguments are passed  many arguments which you want to pass in bulk  how are they passed

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: let's get started  Code Separation  we all know that function is used to separate code  and we can repeat it as many times as we want  today I will create a function

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: and I will name it as average  and I will print  and I will print  the average is  and I will write here

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: a plus b by 2  now you have to remember one thing  that if you are doing calculation like this  then you should know that  which calculation you want to do first

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: in this case if I show you  I want to do a plus b by 2 first  and after that I want to do  this by 2  so I will run this program

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: so you can see here  nothing happened  why nothing happened  because I have given two numbers  which I have to calculate the average

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: and obviously I will do a comma b  and now I will run as you can see  the average is 5.0  it is printed here  so this is a simple function

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: there are four types of arguments  that we can provide in a function  default arguments, keyword arguments  variable length arguments  and required arguments

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: in this case a and b are required arguments  now let's talk about default arguments  I can give a value  9 and b value  1 by default

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: in this case if I run the average  I will run the average like this  then it will work  and it will assume a value as 9 and b value as 1  and give me 5

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: but if I give a value as 1 and b value as 5  then it will ignore 9 and 1  and it will take a and b value respectively  so my answer will be 3  as you can see

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: so these are our default arguments  we can provide a default value while creating a function  this way the function assumes a default value  even if a value is not provided in the function  for that argument

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: now here I want to give a value  and want to put b value as default  so what I will do here  let's say I give 4 or 5  and I run it

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: so you can see I gave a value as 5  and I didn't give b value  so b value will be 1  and 5 plus 1 by 2  6 by 2 means 3 will be printed

chunks.text: if I want to give b value  then I will write something like this  let's say b is equal to 9  a value is 9  b value will be 9

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: and 9.0 will be printed  so I can give any value like this  and the other value  will be taken from the default arguments  so these are the default arguments

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: now here I have given an example  that a function named name is made  first name, middle name, last name  and after that if you give only first name  then your middle name and last name

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: will be from the default arguments  if you give all three  then obviously your first name, middle name  and last name will be from the default arguments  and I want to run this example

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: and see here  when we are printing  hello amy john watson is written  if I write here  amy agarwal

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: then amy agarwal watson  will be printed  you can see amy agarwal watson  if I print  amy agarwal jain

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: then amy agarwal jain will be printed  so we have seen  that amy agarwal jain is printed  now what we will do  I will remove this example

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: because it is already given in rappel  now let's talk about keyword arguments  keyword arguments  are very simple arguments  which you can give like this

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: you can change the order of arguments  like in this case  if I want to give value of b  then I can give value of b as 9  and a is equal to 21

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: and if a is equal to 21  then b is equal to 9  so I don't need to give in that order  so this is how they are used  we can provide arguments with key is equal to value

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: this way the interpreter recognizes the arguments  by the parameter name  hence the order in which the arguments are passed  does not matter  if you want that order doesn't matter

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: if you give arguments by default  then you have to give a first  if you use this kind of syntax  then you don't have to  care about the order

chunks.text: so here this thing is also clear  now see what are required arguments  required arguments are those arguments  which you have to give  now if I don't write a is equal to 9

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: then a will become required argument  you have to give value of a  you can't escape from that  so you have to give value of a  whether you give value of b or not

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: if you give then it will take that value  if you don't give then  it will take the default value of b  simple thing  in case we don't pass the arguments

chunks.title: Function Arguments in Python
chunks.text: it is necessary to pass the arguments  in the correct position order  and the number of arguments passed  should match with actual function definition  this is very important

chunks.text: if here value of a, b and c are taken  suppose 3 values are taken  c is also taken  so I write a, b and c is equal to 1  so c is optional

text: What's up guys, you are back in 100 days of code and today I will tell you about function arguments that how can arguments be passed to function we said that function is used to separate code but there are many ways by which we can pass arguments to function we can pass some arguments to function as a tuple and we can pass as a dictionary let's go to the computer screen and see how default arguments are passed many arguments which you want to pass in bulk how are they passed let's get started Code Separation we all know that function is used to separate code and we can repeat it as many times as we want today I will create a function and I will name it as average and I will print and I will print the average is and I will write here a plus b by 2 now you have to remember one thing that if you are doing calculation like this then you should know that which calculation you want to do first in this case if I show you I want to do a plus b by 2 first and after that I want to do this by 2 so I will run this program so you can see here nothing happened why nothing happened because I have given two numbers which I have to calculate the average and obviously I will do a comma b and now I will run as you can see the average is 5.0 it is printed here so this is a simple function there are four types of arguments that we can provide in a function default arguments, keyword arguments variable length arguments and required arguments in this case a and b are required arguments now let's talk about default arguments I can give a value 9 and b value 1 by default in this case if I run the average I will run the average like this then it will work and it will assume a value as 9 and b value as 1 and give me 5 but if I give a value as 1 and b value as 5 then it will ignore 9 and 1 and it will take a and b value respectively so my answer will be 3 as you can see so these are our default arguments we can provide a default value while creating a function this way the function assumes a default value even if a value is not provided in the function for that argument now here I want to give a value and want to put b value as default so what I will do here let's say I give 4 or 5 and I run it so you can see I gave a value as 5 and I didn't give b value so b value will be 1 and 5 plus 1 by 2 6 by 2 means 3 will be printed if I want to give b value then I will write something like this let's say b is equal to 9 a value is 9 b value will be 9 and 9.0 will be printed so I can give any value like this and the other value will be taken from the default arguments so these are the default arguments now here I have given an example that a function named name is made first name, middle name, last name and after that if you give only first name then your middle name and last name will be from the default arguments if you give all three then obviously your first name, middle name and last name will be from the default arguments and I want to run this example and see here when we are printing hello amy john watson is written if I write here amy agarwal then amy agarwal watson will be printed you can see amy agarwal watson if I print amy agarwal jain then amy agarwal jain will be printed so we have seen that amy agarwal jain is printed now what we will do I will remove this example because it is already given in rappel now let's talk about keyword arguments keyword arguments are very simple arguments which you can give like this you can change the order of arguments like in this case if I want to give value of b then I can give value of b as 9 and a is equal to 21 and if a is equal to 21 then b is equal to 9 so I don't need to give in that order so this is how they are used we can provide arguments with key is equal to value this way the interpreter recognizes the arguments by the parameter name hence the order in which the arguments are passed does not matter if you want that order doesn't matter if you give arguments by default then you have to give a first if you use this kind of syntax then you don't have to care about the order so here this thing is also clear now see what are required arguments required arguments are those arguments which you have to give now if I don't write a is equal to 9 then a will become required argument you have to give value of a you can't escape from that so you have to give value of a whether you give value of b or not if you give then it will take that value if you don't give then it will take the default value of b simple thing in case we don't pass the arguments it is necessary to pass the arguments in the correct position order and the number of arguments passed should match with actual function definition this is very important if here value of a, b and c are taken suppose 3 values are taken c is also taken so I write a, b and c is equal to 1 so c is optional if you are giving a or b without key is equal to value then you have to give like this suppose 5, 6 so value of a is 5 and value of b is 6 and here I will comment this function because I have to give 3 values so average is 6.0 value of a is 5, value of b is 6 and default value of c is taken so this is how you can do now you have to run this and tell me whether you could do it successfully or not, whether you understood things or not now let's see about variable length argument so what happens is you can give variable length argument like suppose I have to get average of many numbers now I can get average of 3 numbers, 4 numbers I can get average of 10 numbers so what I will do I will do something like this I will do numbers I will comment this and I will write it again I will comment this also and I will write this function again so I will write here star numbers now numbers is an iterable for i in numbers and I will write sum is equal to sum plus i and finally what I will do that I will write print average is and I will write here something like this average is sum divided by length of numbers and I will initialize sum from 0 something like this and finally if I will do average 5, 6 so it is giving me 5.5 so what happened is it took numbers as a tuple if I print here type of numbers so I will get tuple so it will take as a tuple so it will take tuple so I hope you are getting it how it is working behind the scenes what is happening is I am getting a tuple of numbers means I am getting a list of numbers so we will see in the future what is the difference in tuple list and how we can use it but for now you can understand that we have looped on it and we have added all the numbers and I will print c so I will get this value here so basically what is this average it is computing average of all numbers and it is storing it in c so what happens by return so when you return it will be come in c so you will get 4.75 if I don't return this so you will get none because this function is not returning so you will see none but I want to return this value so if I return 7 so what will I return in else so whatever return you got earlier that value will be finalized and this function will return, return means return this value, what does it mean everyone write in the comment, return means return this value, so this average function will return 7 value will give to c and print c will give 7, but that's not what we want, we want sum divided by len of numbers that's why we want 4.75 so we are getting 4.75 you can see here average 5671 we printed 4.75, so return statement is used to return the value of the expression back to the calling function calling function in this case is average it gets back the value which value? this value which average function has computed sum by len of numbers because it is 4.75 I hope you understood this, you can read this again and if you find
